calendar.description: House Economic Development and Workforce Services Hearing
Senate Government Operations and Political Subdivisions Hearing

general_provisions: This bill modifies provisions related to public funds.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>changes the name of:<hr><ltbullet1>the Agriculture Conservation Easement Account; and<hr><ltbullet1>the LeRay McAllister Working Farm and Ranch Fund;<hr><ltbullet>repeals the following accounts and funds, including related references:<hr><ltbullet1>the Navajo Water Rights Negotiation Account;<hr><ltbullet1>the Conversion to Alternative Fuel Grant Program Fund, including the Conversion to Alternative Fuel Grant Program;<hr><ltbullet1>the Wildlife Resources Conservation Easement Account;<hr><ltbullet1>the Wild Game Meat Donation Fund; and<hr><ltbullet1>the Colorado River Authority Restricted Account;<hr><ltbullet>modifies the permissible uses of funds in the Department of Public Safety Restricted Account;<hr><ltbullet>clarifies that an agency that administers a state grant on another agency's behalf shall comply with the applicable grant requirements;<hr><ltbullet>clarifies that the state auditor may audit grant funds in accordance with the state auditor's authority;<hr><ltbullet>for a direct award grant, prohibits an administering agency from using grant funds to administer the grant, unless otherwise provided in the grant appropriation's intent language;<hr><ltbullet>modifies certain reporting requirements for a competitive grant;<hr><ltbullet>provides that a member of the Board of Examiners is disqualified from reviewing a line item overexpenditure report if the line item is part of the member's office's budget;<hr><ltbullet>allows an agency to expend up to 100% of the dedicated credits revenue that the agency receives in excess of the amount appropriated, if the dedicated credits are appropriated to a specified type of fund;<hr><ltbullet>amends the administration of the Industrial Assistance Account (account) by:<hr><ltbullet1>replacing the annual transfer to the account with an annual set aside; and<hr><ltbullet1>directing the GOEO board to make recommendations to the administrator regarding applications for loans, grants, or other financial assistance from the account;<hr><ltbullet>addresses the state auditor's authority related to:<hr><ltbullet1>expenses and personnel; and<hr><ltbullet1>performing audits of funds and accounts to determine compliance with the law;<hr><ltbullet>creates the Energy Development Infrastructure Fund to make loans to public entities to finance infrastructure development that supports nuclear power generation and transmission in the state;<hr><ltbullet>changes the Electrical Energy Development Investment Fund from an expendable special revenue fund to a special revenue fund and makes appropriations from the fund nonlapsing;<hr><ltbullet>modifies the permissible uses of the Electrical Energy Development Investment Fund;<hr><ltbullet>makes technical and conforming changes; and<hr><ltbullet>includes a coordination clause that makes technical changes if this bill and H.B. 473, Colorado River Authority Amendments, both pass and become law.
